title: Java LocalDate Duration
description: Practice LocalDate and Duration from java.time.

completionChecks.prompt: What does due hold when start is 2026-05-18?
completionChecks.explanation: plusDays(14) adds 14 days to May 18; May has 31 days, so the date rolls into June as 2026-06-01.

completionChecks.prompt: What does start hold after start.plusDays(14) runs?
choices: 2026-05-18, because LocalDate is immutable and plusDays returns a new date
2026-06-01, because plusDays mutates start in place
null, until the result is assigned back
Compilation fails because LocalDate has no plusDays method
completionChecks.explanation: java.time types are immutable; plusDays returns a new LocalDate (assigned to due) and leaves start unchanged at 2026-05-18.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if code calls start.plusDays(14); without assigning the result?
completionChecks.explanation: LocalDate is immutable, so plusDays returns a new date and leaves the original start reference unchanged unless reassigned.
